content:
# Mark Chapter 13 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (Aleph, 01), folios 39r–39v (PDF pages 78–79) | **Source Image:** `source-texts/greek-NT/MRK_013_page_001.jpg`, `source-texts/greek-NT/MRK_013_page_002.jpg`
**Secondary Witness:** Codex Vaticanus (B, 03) — consulted via [DigiVatLib](https://digi.vatlib.it/view/MSS_Vat.gr.1209)

---

¹ And as-he-was-going-out of the temple, one of-his disciples says to-him, "Teacher, look! What stones and what buildings!"

² And Jesus said to-him, "Do-you-see these great buildings? There-will not be-left stone upon stone which will not be-thrown-down."

³ And as-he-was-sitting on the Mount of Olives opposite the temple, Peter and James and John and Andrew were-asking him privately,

⁴ "Tell us, when will these-things be? And what [will be] the sign when all these-things are-about to-be-accomplished?"

⁵ And Jesus began to-say to-them, "Watch-out that no-one leads-you-astray.

⁶ Many will-come in my name, saying, 'I am [he],' and they-will-lead many astray.

⁷ But when you-hear of-wars and rumors of-wars, do not be-alarmed. It-must happen, but the end [is] not yet.

⁸ For nation will-rise against nation and kingdom against kingdom. There-will-be earthquakes in various places; there-will-be famines. These [are the] beginning of-birth-pains.

⁹ But watch yourselves. They-will-deliver you to councils, and in synagogues you-will-be-beaten, and before governors and kings you-will-stand for-the-sake-of me, for a-testimony to-them.

¹⁰ And first it-is-necessary [for] the gospel to-be-proclaimed to all the nations.

¹¹ And when they-lead you [away], delivering [you], do not worry-beforehand what you-might-speak, but whatever is-given to-you in that hour, speak this; for it-is not you speaking but the Holy Spirit.

¹² And brother will-deliver brother to death, and father child, and children will-rise-up against parents and will-put them-to-death.

¹³ And you-will-be hated by all because-of my name. But the-one having-endured to [the] end — this-one will-be-saved.

¹⁴ But when you-see the abomination of-desolation standing where it-must not — let the-one reading understand — then let-those in Judea flee to the mountains.

¹⁵ And let the-one on the housetop not come-down nor enter to-take anything from his house.

¹⁶ And let the-one in the field not turn back to-take his garment.

¹⁷ But woe to-those having [a child] in [the] womb and to-those nursing in those days!

¹⁸ But pray that it-might not happen in winter.

¹⁹ For those days will-be tribulation such-as has not been from [the] beginning of-creation which God created until now, and will not be.

²⁰ And if [the] Lord had not shortened the days, no flesh would-have-been-saved. But for-the-sake-of the elect whom he-chose, he-shortened the days.

²¹ And then if anyone says to-you, 'Look, here [is] the Christ!' or 'Look, there!' — do not believe [it].

²² For false-christs and false-prophets will-arise and will-give signs and wonders, so-as to-lead-astray, if possible, the elect.

²³ But you, watch-out! I-have-told you all-things-beforehand.

²⁴ But in those days, after that tribulation, the sun will-be-darkened and the moon will not give its light,

²⁵ and the stars will-be falling from heaven, and the powers in the heavens will-be-shaken.

²⁶ And then they-will-see the Son of Man coming in clouds with great power and glory.

²⁷ And then he-will-send the angels and will-gather his elect from the four winds, from [the] end of-earth to [the] end of-heaven.

²⁸ But from the fig-tree learn the parable: when its branch already becomes tender and puts-forth the leaves, you-know that the summer is near.

²⁹ So also you, when you-see these-things happening, know that he-is near, at [the] doors.

³⁰ Truly I-say to-you that this generation will not pass-away until all these-things happen.

³¹ Heaven and earth will-pass-away, but my words will not pass-away.

³² But concerning that day or the hour, no-one knows — neither the angels in heaven nor the Son — except the Father.

³³ Watch-out, be-alert! For you-do not know when the time is.

³⁴ [It is] as a-man going-abroad, having-left his house and having-given to-his servants the authority, to-each his work, and having-commanded the doorkeeper that he-might-watch.

³⁵ Watch therefore — for you-do not know when the lord of-the house comes, whether at-evening or at-midnight or at-cock-crowing or early-morning —

³⁶ lest having-come suddenly, he-finds you sleeping.

³⁷ And what I-say to-you, I-say to-all: Watch!"

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **13:10 — "first the gospel must be proclaimed to all the nations":** This verse interrupts the flow of the persecution warnings (vv. 9, 11). Some scholars consider it a Markan insertion; others see it as integral. Sinaiticus preserves it clearly in sequence.

2. **13:14 — "the abomination of desolation":** ΤΟ ΒΔΕΛΥΓΜΑ ΤΗΣ ΕΡΗΜΩΣΕΩΣ, from Daniel 9:27; 11:31; 12:11. The neuter noun (βδέλυγμα) takes a masculine participle (ΕΣΤΗΚΟΤΑ — "standing") in Sinaiticus — a grammatical incongruity that may be deliberate, suggesting a person rather than a thing.

3. **13:14 — "let the one reading understand":** This parenthetical — ὁ ἀναγινώσκων νοείτω — is an editorial aside, possibly from Mark himself or from the tradition he received. It addresses the reader directly, breaking the fourth wall.

4. **13:32 — "nor the Son":** Sinaiticus includes ΟΥΔΕ Ο ΥΙΟΣ — "nor the Son." This is theologically significant: Jesus explicitly claims ignorance of the timing of the end. Some later manuscripts omit this phrase, presumably to protect Jesus's omniscience.

5. **13:33 — "Be alert" (ΑΓΡΥΠΝΕΙΤΕ):** Sinaiticus reads ΑΓΡΥΠΝΕΙΤΕ ("be alert/watchful"). Some manuscripts add ΚΑΙ ΠΡΟΣΕΥΧΕΣΘΕ ("and pray"). I follow Sinaiticus.

— EMET, AI Translator
